preflight_amendment: Before a completed arm, native-Codex preflights showed that the outer macOS sandbox needed process-fork for runtime threads, the compatibility page-size sysctl used by Rust stack initialization, metadata-only visibility of the absent managed requirements path, a finite set of named macOS startup services, the exact DNS/TLS services and public resolver files used by Codex's own macOS network policy, explicit access to notification-center shared memory, and the canonical /private/var/run/mDNSResponder path that Seatbelt evaluates behind the /var/run firmlink. The broad network allow was replaced by the Chromium-style boundary: the one resolver socket plus remote TCP/UDP. Native Codex creates and locks CODEX_HOME/installation_id during app-server startup, so the runner stages only bounded auth.json and optional models_cache.json bytes into a fresh writable per-arm home and removes that home in a finally block; the user's real credential directory stays read-only and is not retained. The first network-enabled launch reached TLS root loading but returned no HTTP or model response because rustls-native-certs attempted the denied macOS keychain path. Commit 8a03d220fe39e644189433a2bc8636ce8c3b95a9 pinned SSL_CERT_FILE to the already allowlisted system PEM bundle and added only the OS metadata sysctls used by uname, avoiding user-keychain access. One later launch exited nonzero without a completed arm; the prior runner did not retain a structured failure diagnostic, so it cannot honestly be classified as before-provider or after-provider. Commit 45163f893592204930125d651ea2f5b21c376fb6 now extracts only bounded, secret-redacted documented JSONL failure events, records stdout and stderr digests rather than raw failure streams, and removes the disposable home used by the version check. The corrected diagnostic then proved that the provider rejected gpt-5.6-sol because the initially pinned Codex 0.139.0 was too old; no final answer or completed arm was produced. The final attempt therefore pins Codex 0.144.5 from OpenAI's rust-v0.144.5 macOS arm64 package. Its downloaded package matched the release SHA-256 8d1cd2d53b2070919d12c054b57485b6e08347e2666cb20932e9e95eb2aa2901, and the extracted binary digest is pinned below. The registered facts, answer schema, requested model, budgets, arms, rubric, and decision rule are unchanged; the prior amendments changed only the runner and then the CLI version required to execute the already registered model request.
purpose: Internal agent proxy for whether inherited Vela state reduces dead-route work and review burden.
same_information_rule: Both prompts are rendered by the registered runner from the same facts object. Treatment changes organization and typed state labels, not facts.
decision_rule: With n=1 per arm, publish raw results and no causal claim. Treatment is only directionally useful if it has no dead route, full downstream reuse, no lower rubric score, and no higher review burden; otherwise simplify the briefing.
